title: Regime-Specialist Ensemble With OOF Sigmoid Calibration
summary: The strongest public pattern here is heterogeneity: dry vs wet, green vs neutralized, early vs late race, and first-stop vs later-stop behavior differ meaningfully.
rationale: The strongest public pattern here is heterogeneity: dry vs wet, green vs neutralized, early vs late race, and first-stop vs later-stop behavior differ meaningfully. A small specialist ensemble can exploit that, while sigmoid calibration preserves ranking unlike isotonic, which can introduce ties and perturb AUC.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for at least dry-slick, wet/intermediate, and late-race finish-window regimes, plus one global model. Blend using OOF predictions only, then calibrate the final blend with fold-safe sigmoid calibration on held-out folds; use isotonic only if a large calibration set exists and AUC loss is acceptable.
expected_effect: Better probability quality and modest AUC lift from regime specialization without overcommitting to one monolithic feature space.
risk: Main trap is stacked leakage: base predictions, blend weights, and calibration all must be generated strictly out of fold. Over-segmentation can also starve minority regimes.
